entry.title: Optimizing Your SAP S/4HANA Landscape with a Best Practice Role Concept
entry.content:
Optimizing your SAP S/4HANA Landscape with a Best Practice Role Concept

Experts familiar with SAP authorizations are well aware that quality issues and complexity of legacy role concepts often prompt the desire to start from scratch with a brand-new concept. However, the thought of the associated effort and high costs discourages many. What if there were a more efficient solution?

Typically, the following topics are initiators for a revision of authorization concepts:

  • SAP S/4HANA implementations in cloud or hybrid environments usually require – following the "keep the core clean" strategy – a new SAP S/4HANA authorization concept as well as an authorization concept for the SAP Business Technology Platform.
  • SAP S/4HANA greenfield implementations for new SAP customers as well as existing SAP customers developing new processes based on SAP S/4HANA require a new authorization concept that fits to their new process design and covers access to SAP Fiori Launchpad
  • SAP S/4HANA brownfield implementations that cause a redesign or at least an update of the existing authorization concept.
  • Audit and compliance requirements that may result in a revision or redesign of the SAP authorization concept.

In this blog post, we introduce the benefits of the “Automated Implementation of Role Concept” Service ("AIRC Service"), which shortens the efforts and duration of the projects by applying pre-defined roles and a high level of automation. Based on a clear methodology, the service has proven to be a key for the success of authorization projects within more than 200 customer projects over the past 15 years.

 

How does the use case look like for the implementation of a new authorization concept?

When developing a new authorization concept, the initial scope is important, i.e. which applications (i.e. Transactions as well as Fiori Apps) should be part of the authorization concept. This is a task in the responsibility of the business departments, but AIRC service supports it by offering the following approaches:

  • "Top-Down Approach" via SAP Signavio Extraction or manual upload:
    •  With the AIRC service, data can be automatically extracted from SAP Signavio. SAP Signavio is the recommended solution for the (re-) design of Business Processes.
    • If other tools for modelling business processes are in place, the data can be exported and used by AIRC Service.

The Top-Down Approach is the preferred method for greenfield implementations.

  • Business Reverse Engineering:
    • By examining the transactions used in the SAP system, which can be extracted and used as a baseline for defining the new role proposal, the AIRC ensures that only the necessary functions are authorized. For customers moving from SAP ECC to SAP S/4HANA or upgrading their SAP S/4HANA, the following analyses are also helpful:
      • SAP S/4HANA Impact Analysis: This analysis checks applications for availability in the target release and lists any corresponding successor applications (transactions or apps).
      • Fiori App Recommendations: This analysis lists which native Fiori apps can complement a defined range of SAP transactions.

“Business Reverse Engineering” is the preferred method for brownfield implementations.

 

Best Practice Role Proposal

Over the years, SAP Services have developed a database consisting of proven best practice roles that meet the following quality criteria:

  • Functional role concept
  • Consideration of typical segregation of duty (SoD) requirements
  • Display and maintenance roles
  • Module-specific roles
  • SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) authorization concept
  • and many more

This database is constantly being expanded based on new applications (e.g. new Fiori apps) and continuously optimized based on customer feedback.

Using the methods described above, we can quickly provide the customer with a customized role proposal from the intersection of the scope and our role database (often already at the project kick-off). We also offer role assignments for customer-specific applications using algorithms (partly AI-based).

The role proposal provides a solid framework that significantly reduces efforts in the business departments, enhances quality, and stipulates a unified format.

 

Role Implementation

The AIRC service considerably supports the implementation of roles through automation, greatly reducing the efforts required for implementation.  The following steps outline the implementation:

  • Creation of roles with corresponding applications: Based on the authorization concept revised by the business departments, PFCG roles and Fiori catalogs, including assignment of transactions and Fiori apps, are generated using the AIRC service tool-based approach.
  • Maintenance of non-organizational authorization fields: The corresponding authorization values in the roles are manually maintained with the help of standard proposals (SU24) and feedback from the business departments.
  • Generation of organizational or non-organizational supplementary roles: Special authorizations require supplementary roles, consisting of distinct authorization objects (examples include release codes, order types, document types, etc.). We generate these roles from Excel files using a tool-based approach.
  • Generation of role derivations: We recommend using the SAP standard derivation concept. For each organization, so-called "orgsets" can be defined, which contain the specifications for the organizational restrictions of that organization (company code, plant, purchasing organization, sales organization, etc.). Role derivations can then be generated using the AIRC service based on these “orgsets”. This step is not feasible to perform manually for larger companies or projects involving around 20, 100, or 200 organizations.
  • Generation of SAP SAP BTP (Business Technology Platform) roles and role collections: In "side-by-side extensions", business data can also end up in the SAP BTP, which can lead to many SAP BTP roles / role collections. The AIRC service offers tools necessary for generating and transporting these roles, protecting you from unpleasant surprises such as significant manual efforts just before Go-Live.

 

Testing roles

For re-design projects, the SAP standard offers a very useful, currently not very well-known feature:

With the transaction "STSIMAUTHCHECK" it is possible to simulate which authorization checks would fail when using new roles instead of old ones. In our experience, for new implementations, the classic method of testing and ad hoc correcting roles individually is one of the most effective. The AIRC service can also be used in conjunction with partner products that support the tests.

 

Assignment of roles to users

If usage data is available, we support the assignment of roles to test users and end users with role assignment proposal, structured according to the following procedure:

  • Allocation of default authorization roles
  • If an application has been used by a user that occurs in different roles, the less critical role is initially suggested.
  • Organizational assignments can be made based on the user's organizational assignments or based on the organizational assignments of the old roles.

If the customer does not have their own Identity Access Management tools, we support the implementation of the assignment with corresponding tools (ABAP and SAP BTP).

 

Go-Live

With the use of "STSIMAUTHCHECK," you can conduct a so called "Protected Go-Live", which is carried out using old authorizations and logging of theoretical authorization errors caused by the new roles. For a re-design, the transition usually occurs in two to four increasingly larger user packages.

 

Do I also get support if I decide to rely on my former role concept?

If there are no major changes in business processes and the authorization concept of the customer is already at a high-quality level, customers might decide not to reimplement the authorization concept with SAP S/4HANA.  For all the new topics (e.g. roles for SAP BTP) the above steps can still be applied. For the existing role concept, the differences between SAP ECC and SAP S/4HANA or between different SAP S/4HANA releases can be derived by an Impact Analysis. We will not further describe this topic in this blog post, please contact us if you want to get further details on this topic.

 

Wrapping It Up: Key Takeaways

The AIRC service addresses the required re-design of permissions within upcoming changes such as cloud transformations, SAP S/4HANA projects, or tightened compliance requirements. With the AIRC service's help, the implementation efforts and runtime of authorization projects can be greatly reduced. You will receive a standardized and well-proven role concept that meets common compliance requirements. The service’s automations also support newer technologies like processing Fiori artifacts and SAP BTP Roles/Role Collections, preventing issues with newer technologies in upcoming projects.

entry.title: SAP Sovereign Cloud Capabilities and What Sets Us Apart
entry.content:
Author – Subhranshu Sekhar

Introduction

In this post, we will explore SAP's sovereign cloud capabilities, what sets us apart, sovereign cloud solutions portfolio, deployment model and the critical aspects of security in SAP’s sovereign Cloud capabilities, as well as best practices to ensure your data remains protected.

This is a first of a series of blogs about SAP’s sovereign cloud capabilities.

SAP's Definition of Sovereign Cloud

SAP’s sovereign cloud capabilities are specialised cloud services designed to meet the unique security, compliance, and data sovereignty requirements of organisations operating in regulated environments. This includes a cloud computing architecture designed to enforce strict compliance with a country’s laws and regulations regarding data residency and data sovereignty. This cloud service ensures that data resides within specific geopolitical boundaries, providing enhanced control and protection against unauthorized access.

Key Differentiators

  • Cloud services align with government standards or policies to support requirements to certify or authorise the services for use
  • Security-cleared personnel manage cloud computing and operations infrastructure
  • Operations take place in government accredited security facilities. 
  • A local control plane and local automation ensure that organisations can handle data self-sufficiently within their country
  • Subsequent self-sufficiency ensures resilience and maintenance of control and security over cloud workloads, no matter the geopolitical circumstances.

Sovereign Cloud Portfolio

The SAP Intelligent Sustainable Enterprise Suite provides next-generation customer experience by delivering intelligent and innovative solutions for every line of business; these are end-to-end business processes that are modular, integrated, and span across different business solutions by infusing Generative AI use cases . In this blog we will be touching some of the key LoB’s within our current sovereign cloud capabilities

Security

SAP's approach to security in the sovereign cloud is fundamentally based around 3 pillars

  1. Build Securely
  2. Run Securely
  3. Act Securely

Some of the key security features in SAP’s sovereign cloud capabilities are:

  • Data sovereignty and localization, including stricter access controls
  • Regulatory compliance and regular audits built to comply with Australian ISM controls and all solutions in scope are already IRAP assessed and operated  out of a secure Zone 3 and Zone 4 facility
  • Enhanced security controls include encryption, multi-factor authentication, role based access control, advanced threat detection and response

Customer workloads across all LOB's are secured and protected using a shared responsibility model where SAP’s sovereign cloud operations are responsible for implementing preventative controls which range from helpful to restrictive. SAP’s sovereign cloud compliance and security is aligned to the NIST Framework

SAP’s Sovereign Cloud Capabilities in S/4 HANA Private Cloud Edition

 

 

As the cornerstone of RISE with SAP,  SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition can safeguard your existing ERP investment while you benefit from the latest innovations and business-wide flexibility. The RISE with SAP offering includes an AI-enabled cloud ERP that's managed and optimized by SAP using a clean core approach. RISE with SAP also offers solutions for optimizing processes, extending and enhancing capabilities, and running sustainably.

SAP’s sovereign cloud capabilities are managed services and deployments of security-hardened SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition solutions designed to adhere to national security and secrecy requirements such as government accreditation, attestation, or certification programs. They are designed for SAP customers who require additional assurances for their cloud workloads and cloud delivery model, including those in the public sector and regulated industries. With sovereign cloud capabilities in SAP S/4 HANA Cloud Private Edition, we provide innovative offerings in a more secure sovereign environment, enabling you to comply with the unique regulations specific to your country.

 

BTP

Built upon a Foundation of Trust

SAP’s sovereign cloud capabilities leverage SAP's Business Technology Platform architecture, a proven and secure solution already trusted by organizations worldwide. We take this robust foundation a step further by implementing additional security measures and controls specifically designed to meet the stringent requirements of Australian and New Zealand government departments and critical infrastructure providers

Unparalleled Security and Local Expertise

Your data is our top priority. Our dedicated, Australia-based team provides support for the platform, ensuring your information always remains secure.  Data is stored across geographically redundant data centres, adhering to the highest security standards.

Up to Date and Available

SAP’s sovereign cloud capabilities are built from the ground up for continuous availability. Most updates are deployed in real-time, minimizing disruption to your operations. We leverage industry-leading monitoring and tools to track performance across all cloud components. In the unlikely event of an issue, we are able to leverage our global support team to quickly react and solve the problem, restoring the system’s functionality.

Global Innovation, Locally Delivered

Our global development teams are constantly working to introduce new features and enhancements to the platform. These advancements undergo rigorous security assessments by our dedicated team before being deployed to your environment by local experts. This ensures you benefit from cutting-edge innovation whilst maintaining the highest security posture.

 

 

HCM

 SAP SuccessFactors HCM is a truly global suite of cloud HR solutions that gives you everything you need to fuel employee success and drive organizational agility at scale. Built on SAP’s trusted innovation platform and powered by a single source of people and skills data, it provides the foundation and flexibility required to improve every people experience, decision, and process across your entire business.

Elevate every experience. Increase employee engagement and productivity by delivering individualized experiences designed for how, when, and where people work.

Create a future ready workforce. Boost performance and retention by finding, developing, and mobilizing the skills, attributes, and leaders needed for today and tomorrow.

Enable agile and compliant HR. Improve organizational agility by supporting all people in all locations across all ways of working with the flexibility to quickly expand and adapt.

Increase the strategic value of HR. Achieve more faster with less risk, complexity, and cost by adopting a cloud-first approach backed by technology, partnership, and community.

SAP SuccessFactors offerings within sovereign cloud are designed for SAP customers who require additional assurances for their cloud workloads and cloud delivery model, including those in the public sector and regulated industries. Security-cleared nationals manage cloud computing and operations infrastructure out of certified security facilities.

Our sovereign cloud solutions are designed to meet key audit and regulatory standards.

 

As more offerings and roadmap items are released within SAP sovereign cloud capabilities , future blogs will be covering those updates

This blog is also a contribution from following team members:

Kirsten Tucker – Sovereign cloud ANZ head of Delivery

Hadi Zadeh – Sovereign cloud ANZ ISSO

Warrick Howieson - Sovereign cloud ANZ BTP Core Lead

Bill Xie - Sovereign cloud ANZ BTP Extensions Lead

Abhishek Gudup - Sovereign cloud ANZ HCM Lead

entry.title: SAP Customers Conversations Event: SAP S/4HANA public cloud @ Zelestra
entry.content:
Dear Utilities Community

We are pleased to invite you to our upcoming Customer Conversations LIVE Event with Zelestra about their Grow with SAP journey. In this session Borja Guerrero, Chief Digital Officer from our customer Zelestra is going to discuss how they leverage the capabilities of SAP to sustain growth.

In this webinar, our experts will discuss advanced features and best practices for SAP covering a range of topics including:

- Project overview
- Benefits gained
- Lessons learned
- Demo of the system
- Q&A session with participants

Whether you are a long-time SAP user looking to optimize your system or you are considering an SAP implementation for the first time, this webinar will provide you with valuable information to help you make the most of your investment.

Date: Wednesday, October 30, 2024

Time: 3:00 PM Central European Time

Duration: 1 hour

 

Please register here

entry.title: Gen AI meets SAP: Improve your sales strategies with SAP S/4HANA, SAP Datasphere and SAC
entry.content:
Introduction

In my previous blog , How Metadata Enrichment can enhance SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine on GenAI scenarios , i focused on leveraging the SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine together with Metadata Enrichment through GenAI, to further extend analytical insights. This blog will focus on combining sales data from SAP S/4HANA with product reviews, centrally stored in SAP Datasphere. Leveraging the powerful analytics capabilities of SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC), we are will generate actionable sales insights that uplift the conventional sales strategies.

This blog  will explore the benefits of combing sentiment analysis (using SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine and Large Language Models [LLM])  and SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP). These cutting-edge technologies together enhance sales and market strategies. These tools, can help businesses transition from basic descriptive analyses to advanced predictive and prescriptive insights, thereby unlocking  significant market opportunities. We'll examine how sentiment analysis reveals customer sentiment from product reviews, while LLMs and SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine facilitate the processing and contextualization of vast data sets. Integration solutions,part of SAP BTP, ensure seamless data flow and scalability, as well as real-time responsiveness. 

Integrating Sales Data with Product Review Sentiments

The first step is to integrate sales data from SAP S/4HANA into SAP Datasphere. At the same time, we will take advantage of Generative AI (Gen AI) to analyze product reviews, applying sentiment analysis to understand customer opinions and trends. These reviews, enriched with sentiment scores and categorized against segments of interest (such as product, supplier or delivery experience), are then ingested into SAP Datasphere. This approach harmonizes structured and unstructured data; and also prepares the data for advanced, multi-dimensional analytics.

By combining transactional data with customer sentiments, businesses can gain valuable insights using SAP Analytics Cloud. This integration allows companies to use years of logged data to identify areas for improvement such as enhancing customer support and optimizing delivery services. This data-driven approach helps businesses shift from reactive decision-making to proactive strategies that meet market needs and improve customer satisfaction.

Leveraging Generative AI for Sentiment Analysis of Product Reviews ( Using Synthetic Data )

To conduct this analysis, we used an approach similar to the one in the previous blog. However, in this study, we created synthetic data that closely matches the characteristics of the original dataset. Using synthetic data is important because it allows us to test and train our models without compromising the privacy and integrity of the actual data. Nowadays, generating synthetic data is quite simple with generative AI. In order to do this, each product review was converted into a vector on SAP HANA Cloud within SAP Datasphere. We enriched these vectors with the sentiments and information found in the original dataset.

We used Gen AI to:

  • Create reviews that have the same overall sentiment as the original ones.
  • Mention similar issues encountered, such as product delivery and price sensitivity.
  • Address other relevant factors by considering them at different hierarchical levels.

This approach ensures that our synthetic data is both secure and highly functional for analysis

Synthetic dataset regarding product reviews:

  • User's Overall Review (negative, positive, neutral)
  • Delivery Review (On Time, Delayed, Never Delivered ...) 
  • Price Review (Value for Money, Overpriced ...)
  • Supplier Review (Satisfied, Very Disappointed ...)
  • Customer Support (Satisfied, Very Disappointed ...) 
  • Issues Encountered (Breakouts, Missing Brush ...)
  • Issues Resolutions (Price decrease, Product Refund ...)
  • Resolution Level 1 (Delivery, Packaging ...)
  • Resolution Level 2 (Accessories, Leakage ...)
  • Resolution Level 3 (Color Difference, Expiration Date ...)

    The information concerning the issues of resolutions and their resolving levels was too extensive. An additional pass through using GenAI was necessary to organize this data in a more meaningful manner. Additionally, four more summarized pieces of information regarding the reviews were added :

    • Resolution (Improved Packaging, Improved Pricing ...)
    • Level 1 (Customer Support, Safety Concerns ...)
    • Level 2 (Design Issues, Missing Product ...)
    • Level 3 (Broken Parts, Product Quantity ...)

For example, analyzing two product reviews, the following metadata has been extracted:

 

Supplementary Data Sources and Final Integration

Taking into consideration that this is a demo, we limited the available attributes per "Product", "Customer" and "Supplier" entities. The same constraint apply to the Sales Value and Sales Volume metric. By merging sales data from SAP S/4HANA with sentiment analysis within SAP Datasphere, we developed the final analytical model which will be consumed on SAP Analytics Cloud.

The lineage analysis from the SAP Datasphere model is the following:

This model includes the following dimensions: Product, Calendar, Customer and Supplier along with Sales Data.

Transforming Sales Strategy with SAP Analytics Cloud

By leveraging SAC’s advanced analytical capabilities, businesses can now perform multi-faceted analyses to discover hidden patterns and trends on top of the diversified Data Sources.

Sales teams can drill down into regional customer satisfaction and correlate it with supplier efficiency to identify market-specific strategies. Additionally, predictive analytics powered by SAC can forecast future sales trends by analyzing historical data and enriched generative AI insights, allowing for proactive inventory management and targeted marketing campaigns.Moreover, Reviews outlining customer issues may yield insights for further improvement, e.g. improvement in logistics and delivery services.

The synergy between SAC and Gen AI thus transforms static sales reports into a living strategy dashboard. This powerful combination enables a more informed, responsive, and strategic sales approach, strengthening the business’s competitive position in the market.

Introducing the GenAI-Powered SAP Analytic Cloud Dashboard

    1. Sales Overview

This dashboard offers a comprehensive overview of sales data, which can be utilized by different personas within a business to make well-informed decisions. Here are some of the business scenarios, which highlight how various roles can leverage these graphs.

Scenario 1: Sales Performance Optimization

Persona: Sales manager

Objective: Monitor and enhance sales performance.

Analytical Insights:

  • Total sales value, volume, and products: The sales manager can quickly assess the overall sales performance by looking at the total sales value (260,000.00), total volume (3,871), and total products (1,810). This provides a snapshot of the business health.
  • Sales per category and subcategory: By analyzing the sales per category and subcategory, the manager can identify which product lines are performing well and which are underperforming. For instance, "Ready and Cream" appears to be a significant contributor.
  • Top and bottom performing products: This helps in identifying the best-selling products like "Skin Renew" and the least performing ones like "Skin Prep." The manager can then strategize to promote the bottom products or phase them out.

Scenario 2: Customer Segmentation and Targeting

Persona: Marketing team

Objective: Tailor marketing campaigns based on customer demographics.

Actionable Insights:

  • Average sales per customer age group: The marketing team can use this data to understand which age groups are contributing most to sales. For example, the age group 35-44 has the highest average sales. Gen AI can create personalized marketing messages and campaigns targeting specific age groups based on their purchasing behavior. For instance, if Gen AI identifies that the 35-44 age group prefers "Skin Renew," targeted ads can be created for this demographic.
  • Total Sales and Volume per Year/Month: This helps in identifying seasonal trends and planning marketing campaigns accordingly.

Scenario 3: Supplier Relationship Management

Persona: Supply chain manager

Objective: Manage and optimize supplier relationships.

Actionable Insights:

  • Total suppliers and average supplier sentiment per category: The supply chain manager can monitor the performance and sentiment of suppliers. For instance, categories like "Bodycare" and "Skincare" have high sentiment scores, indicating good supplier relationships.
  • Sales per product subcategory: This helps in understanding which subcategories are driving sales and ensuring that suppliers for these subcategories are managed effectively. Gen AI can automate communication with suppliers, ensuring timely updates and addressing any issues promptly. This can improve efficiency and maintain strong supplier relationships.

Scenario 4: Product Development and Innovation

Persona: Product development team

Objective: Innovate and develop new products based on market demand.

Actionable Insights:

  • Top and bottom performing products: By analyzing the top-performing products, the product development team can identify features and attributes that are popular among customers.
  • Sales per category and subcategory: This helps in identifying market trends and gaps, guiding the development of new products that meet customer needs.

Scenario 5: Customer Relationship Management

Persona: Customer relationship manager

Objective: Enhance customer satisfaction and loyalty.

Actionable Insights:

  • Total customers and average sales per customer age group: The customer relationship manager can identify key customer segments and tailor engagement strategies accordingly.
  • Sales per product subcategory: Understanding which products are popular among different customer segments can drive personalized customer interactions and recommendations.

    2. Sales / Sentiment Analysis

 

This dashboard provides a detailed overview of sales and sentiment analysis, segmented by various categories, subcategories, suppliers, and time periods. With user-friendly filters and input controls, it allows users to dive deep into the data based on specific categories, subcategories, suppliers, and time frames. Sentiment analysis is visualized through pie charts that represent customer feelings on price, support, delivery, product, and supplier.

Scenario 1: Product Management and Marketing

Persona: Product or marketing manager

Objective: Identify which products are performing well and which are not. For instance, the "Facecare" product in the "Skin Renew" subcategory has the highest sales ($9,195.00) and a high product rating (4.5). This indicates a successful product that could be further promoted.

Scenario 2: Customer Support Improvement

Persona: Customer support manager

Objective: Sentiment analysis identifies areas that need improvement. The "Customer Support Sentiment" pie chart shows that 46.20% of customers have a negative or very negative sentiment towards support. This is a critical area for improvement in support.

Scenario 3: Supplier Relationship Management

Persona: Supply chain manager

Objective: "Customer Supplier Sentiment" data can be used to evaluate supplier performance. With 52.93% of customers satisfied or very satisfied with suppliers, there is room for improvement.

Scenario 4: Strategic Planning and Forecasting

Persona: Business analyst, strategic planner

Objective: Use the sales trends over time to forecast future sales and plan inventory accordingly. The line graph shows fluctuating sales with peaks around specific dates, indicating potential seasonal trends.

Actionable Insights:

  • Product Rating and Sales: The bar chart shows that "Facecare" and "Shampoo & Conditioner" have high sales and ratings, indicating strong market performance. on the contrary, "Anti-Aging" has lower sales, suggesting a need for marketing efforts or product improvement.
  • Customer Sentiments: The pie charts reveal that while price and delivery sentiments are largely positive, customer support and product sentiments have significant negative feedback. This highlights areas for immediate attention.
  • Product Reviews: The bar chart indicates that "Beauty Tools & Devices" and "Skincare" have the highest volume of reviews, both positive and negative. This suggests these categories are highly engaging but may also have quality issues that need addressing.
  • Customer Demographics: The table shows that the 45-55 age group has the highest sales volume ($75,149.00) and generally positive ratings across all metrics. This demographic could be targeted for loyalty programs or personalized marketing campaigns.

    3. Sales and Sentiment Geo Analysis Overview

This dashboard provides analytical insights, including customer support ratings, price ratings, delivery ratings, supplier ratings, a word cloud of top keywords from product reviews, and a geographical sales distribution map.

Scenario 1: Customer Experience Enhancement

Persona: Customer experience manager

Objective: The customer experience manager can use the dashboard to monitor and improve customer satisfaction.

Actionable Insights:

  • If the delivery rating is low, the manager can investigate logistics and supply chain issues.
  • If the price rating is low, they can consider revising pricing strategies or offering promotions.
  • By analyzing the customer support rating, price rating, delivery rating, and supplier rating, they can identify areas needing improvement.

Scenario 2. Market Expansion Strategy

Persona: Marketing manager

Objective: Use the geographical sales distribution map to identify regions with high and low sales performance.

Actionable Insights:

  • If sales are high in Northern Europe but low in Southern Europe, the manager can allocate more marketing resources to Southern Europe and tailor campaigns to local tastes.
  • Use the word cloud to identify popular product features and emphasize them in marketing materials.

Scenario 3: Product Development and Improvement

Persona: Product manager

Objective: Use the word cloud and customer ratings to gather feedback on product performance and identify areas for improvement.

Actionable Insights:

  • If customers frequently mention "razor" and "blade" in a positive context, the manager can consider expanding the product line to include more shaving products.
  • If "smell" and "oil" are mentioned negatively, they can work on improving the product formulation.
  • if "skin" and "hair" are frequently mentioned, the manager can focus on these aspects in product development.

Scenario 4. Supply Chain Optimization

Persona: Supply chain manager

Objective: Use the delivery rating and geographical sales data to optimize logistics and supply chain operations.

Actionable Insights:

  • If delivery ratings are low in certain regions, the manager can investigate and address bottlenecks in the supply chain.
  • Use sales data to ensure that high-demand regions are adequately stocked.

    4. Detailed Analysis on Issues

Users can explore the complex relationships between product reviews and their corresponding resolution outcomes, segmented by resolution levels ranging from 1 to 3. This tab offers a thorough analysis of how effectively customer issues and reviews have been addressed, providing a detailed view of resolution efficacy. By filtering data based on specific suppliers or products, users can identify patterns and correlations that may affect customer satisfaction and product performance.

Scenario 1: Supplier Performance Analysis

Persona: Supply chain manager

Objective: Identify and address supplier-related issues to improve overall supply chain efficiency.

Actionable Insights:

  • Bar Chart Analysis: The "Nbr of Resolution per Supplier (Top 10)" chart shows that "GlamGlow Goods Delivery" has the highest number of resolutions (287), followed by "PoshParcel" (284) and "BeautyBeam Carriers" (278). This indicates that these suppliers are frequently involved in issues that require resolution.
  • Flow Diagram Analysis: By examining the flow diagram, the Supply Chain Manager can trace specific issues back to these suppliers. For instance, if "GlamGlow Goods Delivery" is frequently associated with "Packaging Problems" or "Delivery Delays," these insights can be used to negotiate better terms or seek alternative suppliers.

Scenario 2: Product Quality Improvement

Persona: Product manager

Objective: Enhance product quality and customer satisfaction by addressing common product-related issues.

Actionable Insights:

  • Bar Chart Analysis: The "Nbr of Resolution per Product (Top 10)" chart highlights that "Skin Revive" and "Hair Revive" have the highest number of resolutions (48 and 41 respectively). This suggests that these products are frequently problematic.
  • Flow Diagram Analysis: The Product Manager can use the flow diagram to identify specific issues associated with these products. For example, "Skin Revive" might be linked to "Product Quality Issues" or "Application Difficulties."

Scenario 3: Customer Support Optimization

Persona: Customer support manager

Objective: Streamline customer support processes and improve resolution times.

Actionable Insights:

  • Bar Chart Analysis: The charts provide insights into which products and suppliers are generating the most issues, allowing the Customer Support Manager to allocate resources more effectively.
  • Flow Diagram Analysis: The flow diagram helps in understanding the complexity and interdependencies of various issues. For example, issues like "Instructions Clarity" and "Packaging Problems" might be common across multiple products and suppliers. GENAI can assist in automating responses to common issues, reducing the workload on customer support teams. Furthermore, by analyzing customer feedback, GENAI can provide insights into customer sentiment, helping the support team to prioritize issues that have the most significant impact on customer satisfaction.

    5. Interacting with GPT on Vectorised Documents

Various personas can utilize this interactive widget on the SAC dashboard to pose specific queries. These queries are converted into vector format, matched against Vectorised customer reviews stored in the SAP HANA database, and finally summarized by GPT. This approach facilitates a comprehensive understanding of customer sentiments, performance optimization, campaign effectiveness, product-specific feedback, and competitive analysis, ensuring data-driven strategies across various business functions.

Scenario 1: Customer Sentiment Analysis for Product Improvement

Persona: Product manager

Objective: Understand customer feedback and sentiment for product enhancement.

Actionable Insights:

  • Interactive Widget Analysis: The interactive widget in the fifth tab of the SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) dashboard allows the Product Manager to pose open-ended questions such as "Which is the best cream for young men?" Using GenAI, this query is translated into a vector format and matched against stored, vectorized customer reviews in the SAP HANA database. The result is a selection of relevant reviews and a summary generated by GPT, providing clear insights into customer preferences and sentiments. This enables the Product Manager to identify key areas for product improvement and innovation based on real customer feedback.

Scenario 2: Sales Performance Optimization

Persona: Sales manager

Objective: Optimize sales strategies and improve product performance.

Actionable Insights:

  • Interactive Widget Analysis: The sales manager can use the interactive widget to ask questions like "What are the common complaints about our top-selling products?" The Gen AI powered widget translates this query into a vector format and retrieves relevant customer reviews from the SAP HANA database. The GPT-generated summary provides a concise overview of common issues, allowing the sales manager to address these concerns proactively. This insight helps in refining sales strategies and improving customer satisfaction.

Scenario 3: Marketing Campaign Effectiveness

Persona: Marketing professional

Objective: Evaluate the effectiveness of marketing campaigns and understand customer sentiment.

Actionable Insights:

  • Interactive widget analysis: The Marketing Professional can utilize the interactive widget to ask questions such as "How do customers feel about our latest marketing campaign?" The Gen AI integration translates this query into a vector format and matches it with vectorized customer reviews in the SAP HANA database. The resulting summary generated by GPT provides a clear picture of customer sentiment towards the campaign. This insight helps the Marketing Professional to assess the campaign's impact and make data-driven decisions for future marketing strategies.

Scenario 4: Product-Specific Feedback Analysis

Persona: Quality assurance manager

Objective: Gather detailed feedback on specific products for quality improvement.

Actionable Insights:

  • Interactive Widget Analysis: The Quality assurance manager can use the interactive widget to ask specific questions like "What are the most common issues with our new skincare line?" The Gen AI powered widget translates this query into a vector format and retrieves relevant customer reviews from the SAP HANA database. The GPT-generated summary highlights the most common issues, providing the quality assurance manager with actionable insights to improve product quality and address customer concerns effectively.

Scenario 5: Competitive Analysis

Persona: Business analyst

Objective: Analyze competitor products and customer reviews for strategic planning.

Actionable Insights:

  • Interactive Widget Analysis: The business analyst can leverage the interactive widget to ask questions such as "How do our products compare to our competitors' in terms of customer satisfaction?" The Gen AI integration translates this query into a vector format and matches it with vectorized customer reviews from both the company's and competitors' products stored in the SAP HANA database. The GPT-generated summary provides a comparative analysis of customer satisfaction, helping the Business Analyst to identify strengths and weaknesses and develop strategic plans to gain a competitive edge.

    6. Customer 360

This tab showcases an Customer 360 Dashboard, integrating GenAI and analytics to provide a comprehensive view of customer interactions and sentiments. This powerful combination enables businesses to extract actionable insights, anticipate customer needs, and customize personalized experiences, driving increased customer satisfaction and loyalty.

Scenario 1: Customer Sentiment Analysis for Product Improvement

Persona: Product manager

Objective: Understand customer feedback and sentiment for product enhancement.

Scenario 2: Proactive Customer Support

Persona: Customer support agent

Objective: Provide personalized and informed customer support. The support agent uses the sentiment analysis and issue resolution data to reassure the customer about the price adjustment and highlight the product's value for money. For instance, if a customer contacts support with a query about the "BlendMaster" product, the agent can leverage the positive sentiment towards product quality and the resolved price issue to build trust and satisfaction.

Scenario 3: Targeted Marketing Campaigns

Persona: Marketing manager

Objective: Design effective marketing campaigns based on customer sentiment. Using the sentiment data, the marketing team identifies that customers appreciate the "TrimPro" product's reliability and wet trimming experience. The campaign can focus on these features to attract similar customer segments, leading to higher engagement and conversion rates.

Technical Coffee Corner

Let's explore the underlying architecture that empowers this sales strategy ecosystem. At the heart of this setup is the SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine, which excels in handling high-dimensional vector data by creating a rich vector space representation for unstructured data like product reviews. When unified with the SAP Datasphere, this sophisticated engine facilitates efficient data ingestion, transformation, and harmonization, bridging the gap between isolated SAP S/4HANA transactional data and sentiment-driven insights. To enhance this further, SAP BTP plays a pivotal role in orchestrating these advanced analytics workflows, ensuring seamless data integration, real-time processing, and scalability. Leveraging the power of Large Language Models (LLM), the system employs fine-tuned AI models to contextualize and enrich product reviews, breaking them down into granular sentiment scores and categorized themes. These enriched insights are then fed into SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC), where the robust analytics capabilities and interactive dashboards take center stage. SAC's connectivity and integration layers enable smooth data transfers, while its advanced algorithms enable predictive and prescriptive analytics, providing a multi-dimensional view of sales metrics. The cooperation of these platforms not only elevates the data processing capabilities but also equips businesses to generate forward-looking insights. For any help or clarification, you may contact us at @DKK  , @Dimitrios_Lyras .

Conclusion

Integrating Gen AI with SAP S/4HANA, SAP Datasphere, and SAP Analytics Cloud offers substantial benefits for both IT and business personas. For IT professionals, this combination simplifies the management of complex data landscapes, ensuring robust data security while enhancing the scalability of analytics tasks. With advanced analytical models in SAP Analytics Cloud, IT teams can more easily manage multiple data sources, thereby improving operational efficiency. Business users—including sales managers, marketing teams, product developers, and customer support agents—gain critical insights that drive strategic decision-making. The deep sentiment analysis enhances personalized marketing campaigns, improves customer support, and optimizes supply chain interactions. Utilizing these insights helps organizations shift from reactive to proactive strategies, significantly boosting sales performance, enhancing customer satisfaction, and increasing overall market responsiveness. This comprehensive approach ensures that both IT and business stakeholders can achieve their goals more effectively, leading to sustained business growth and a decisive competitive advantage.

entry.title: SAP Field Service Management high level configurations
entry.content:
How to create a company in FSM cloud?

Create FSM Company with FSM Admin Portal: Please refer the link by Proaxia to create a company in FSM without the E4C Cloud Manager Application i.e., directly in FSM Admin Portal- Create FSM Companies - FSM Cloud Connector - Confluence (atlassian.net)

Once a company is Created, we have a set of configurations available in Admin and Shell application. To configure all these settings, one must be having the role of Admin in cloud account.

  • Steps to complete after the company is created: 

1. Translations

The Translations tab is used to create and manage custom translations that can be used in different places in the application, such as in screen configurations, in business rule actions, or even in widgets for a given company. The Translations tab can be accessed by navigating to Admin > Company > Translations. Here we can create new / Download and upload existing from any other existing companies.

2. Company settings

Here we can customize set of company settings as per the requirements changing the values inside each of them. Also, we can create new / Download and upload existing from any other existing companies.

3. Business rules

We have set of sample business rules provided by SAP which can be used as per requirements, and we can create custom rules which would be required for the complex scenarios. These can be Downloaded and uploaded to any other existing companies as required.

4. Screen Configurations

By default, SAP offers default screen configurations for the several screens within SAP Field Service Management. These you can then use and customize. For each customized configurations, a new version is created and displayed in the screen configurations list. Additionally, the customized configurations are marked accordingly:

5. Service workflow

This is one of the important configurations in which we will see the workflow step in FSM Mobile Application. This allows us to activate and configure checkpoints or milestones for the mobile application in order to standardize processes and improve communications between back-office teams and field employees. The Service Workflow feature can be reached from Admin > Company > Service Workflow. All users with access to the admin app can access this feature.

Sample custom workflow with external applications:

6. Custom objects

The Custom Objects screen is used to create custom fields and objects in order to extend SAP Field Service Management. With Custom Data Objects and Custom Fields, you can create custom objects and fields to fit scenarios that more closely align with your own business needs, and store data in the cloud for automation, validation, dashboards and reporting purposes. In addition, you can also set the classification level for custom field definitions to ensure personal and sensitive data remains secure. These custom objects can then be used in Business Rules create and update object actions. The Custom Objects Definition screen is accessed at Admin > Company > Custom Object Definitions. Custom objects and custom fields are associated with a given company.

Sample: Creating new custom field definitions as Mandatory Off.

 

7. Smartforms / Checklist 

Smartforms can be pre-filled using the key and a corresponding value as defined in the template’s XML file.

The following fields are smartform fields are supported in Deep Links:

  • Text Input
  • Dropdown
  • Date and Time input
  • Number input
  • Checkbox
  • Picker

Go to Shell Application and click on Smart forms and feedback.

Click on Templates and all the created checklists would appear.

Here we can create new or upload and use existing checklists in multiple companies.

Example:

Open Shell w.r.t the new company and follow the same steps as above and click on the + sign in the bottom right corner. 

Note- For the new company, this checklist would not be appearing and would be empty as this is a reference image from the reference company. 

Click on 3 dots where, there would be edit option in the reference company. 

Copy the category name  

Go the newly created company’s tab, click on the + sign to create a new category and paste the category name from the reference company.  

Follow the same steps for other 2 checklists as well. Now that, checklist categories are created, we can download and upload the checklist template. 

In Reference company tab, Double click on the checklist and from the 3 dots, click on export XML, which will download the checklist. 

In new company tab, as the new category is created, upload the template. 

After uploading the template, the template would appear in the new category.

Double click on that and a page like this will open, release the template. 

Note- For the newly created template we have to release as shown below.  

That's it we are ready with the checklist to be utilized in FSM.

 

8. Settings and configurations

Here we have bunch of customizing options to be configured as per client and Admin requirements. The master data is also managed in the same place, for more info regarding data loading: click here

That's it we are good to go and use Field service management in a beginner level.

 

Thanks

Best Regards

Dhanush

entry.title: Consuming SAP with SAP Build Apps - creating a CRUD application
entry.content:
This blog post is the eighth in a series of posts that cover the connectivity options available for SAP Build Apps to interface with SAP systems. The series will cover connecting SAP Build Apps with the following:

  1. CAP-based services
  2. S/4HANA systems
  3. ABAP systems

The format of this post is similar to a tutorial. However, I'll provide more comprehensive details, tips and the opportunity for you to provide feedback. Based on the feedback we will be able to adjust and enhance this post and future parts of the series, and might even be able to improve the products involved as well. 

Introduction 

Before we get into the details of setting up connections between SAP Build Apps and SAP Systems, I intend to introduce a couple of SAP technology components in each post that are relevant for the use cases covered in the series.

CRUD operations

The acronym CRUD stands for Create, Read, Update, Delete. CRUD operations refer to the four basic functions of persistent storage. In the context of OData (Open Data Protocol), these operations are used to interact with data services.

Take note that when interacting with a data service, you are not only storing or retrieving data. The service can also implement (business) logic that is executed when receiving such requests. E.g. for data validation, or triggering another process. Besides that, when looking at access control discussed in part 2 of this blog series, we can configure access to these operations individually for each user.

Use case H : creating a CRUD application in SAP Build Apps

The application we have built so far in this series only shows lists of information. There is no way for the user to create new records, update existing records, or delete records. In this blog post we will change the application we've built in SAP Build Apps into a so-called CRUD application, allowing users to make change to the data stored in the database.

Assuming we are starting a new development session, we open the SAP Build lobby.

As visible in the list of SAP Build projects, we now have 2 versions of the UI application created in SAP Build Apps. Let's open this by selecting the link.

SAP Build Apps opens, and notifies us that there are updates in the marketplace. This is due to a recent release of updates that include components released in the marketplace.

Clicking on the notification brings us to marketplace updates.

Select INSTALL UPDATES to retrieve the latest updates for the flow functions covering create, update and delete a record and SAVE your updated project. These updates are actually quite relevant for this use case!

We can test the current app in Preview to see whether these changes had any impact on our app. When selecting Preview, you will be able to pick the version. We'll pick the latest.

Just as to showcase the starting point of our development, here is a screenshot of the current web preview. It is a very basic list without pagination.

Notice that I've deployed some more initial sample data for the service created in SAP Build Code. This will help while testing the page, for which will also implement the data pagination.

Update the data entity used in our project

Before we get started, we need to update our project to the latest changes in the data service. When we started developing the application, the data entity Products only contained the properties ID, name and price. In the previous blog post we have added a relationship to Supplier, which is stored in a S/4HANA Cloud system.

To update the project, simply navigate to the DATA tab in SAP Build Apps and select the OData integration for Products. The Data entities overview will show the additional field a_Supplier_Supplier, which we've added. However, this simply reflects the current information retrieved from the data service. This might change over time, as new versions of the service are released. Your project in SAP Build Apps does not automatically update to reflect these changes.

To update your project, select DISABLE DATA ENTITY, followed by ENABLE DATA ENTITY for the data entity Products, to ensure the newly added property Supplier is included in your project.

 

Creating a new page to replace the existing one

Instead of changing the existing starting page of the app, we will start by creating a new one. This allows you to refer to a fully functioning page, while we create a replacement. In case you misconfigured something, you can easily check the old page.

Navigate to the pages overview and select ADD NEW PAGE. Provide a name for the page. In this case, I am assigning it the name "Products List", which is going to replace the page "Blog Demo App".

Once the UI canvas editor opens, switch to the VARIABLES and navigate to the DATA VARIABLES tab. Select ADD DATA VARIABLE and pick the Products entity. Rename the data variable to ProductsList. Open the logic editor and connect the Page focused event to the Get record collection. Whenever a user navigates to this page, we will refresh the record collection. This ensures we see the latest updates reflected in the list.


We also add Page Variables to handle the pagination. This will be similar to what we created in blog episode 4, but we'll use a different UI control here and a slightly different configuration.


Adding the list control and page navigation

Switching back to UI Canvas View, we will add list item, navigation buttons and a text field to indicate the page number.  

To prevent the user from navigating to invalid pages, we'll make use of the Disabled property on the buttons. For the left button, we control the property with the following formula.

 

IF(pageVars.CurrentPage>1, false, true)

 

For the right button, we'll disable it to prevent the user from navigating beyond the last page. For this, configure the Disabled property and bind it to the formula:

 

IF(pageVars.CurrentPage<pageVars.TotalPageCount, false, true)

 

For the displaying the current and total amount of pages, we simply bind the Content property to the formula

 

"Page: "+pageVars.CurrentPage + " of "+pageVars.TotalPageCount

 


We'll need to add some logic to both buttons to increase or decrease the current page number respectively. Select the button and open the logic canvas.


For the left button, we set the page variable CurrentPage as follows:

 

pageVars.CurrentPage-1

 

Since we disable the button once CurrentPage is 1, we can keep this formula simple.

For the right button, we set the page variable CurrentPage as follows:

 

pageVars.CurrentPage+1

 


After updating the CurrentPage variable, we configure "Get record collection" with pagination as below.

And after this, we will set the data variable ProductsList to the Output value of the previous node.

We apply the above for the both buttons.


Getting the total amount of records

The page variable TotalPageCount is based on the total amount of records in the database divided by the amount of items we display per page. In this example we will use a fixed number of 10 items per page. You could implement more sophisticated paging that fills the visible screen or window, but we'll keep it simple here.


Select the page canvas and open the logic editor. We will get the record collection of Products, followed by setting the page variable TotalPageCount as follows:

 

INTEGER((outputs["Get record collection"].totalCount -1) / 10) + 1

 

The formula uses the output of "Get record collection" to find out the total amount of records. To prevent loading all data, adding unnecessary overhead and even potential crashes, we need to customise this data request. We configure the property Paging of Get record collection. For the binding, select Object with properties, configured as shown below.

Since we don't need to load any data to retrieve the total record count, we will set the page size and page number to zero. What's relevant for now, is to Include total count.

Switch back to the VARIABLES view and update the logic flow of ProductsList. For Get record collection, we configure Paging.

And we store the result of Get record collection in the ProductsList variable.

 

Adding the new page into the application's navigation flow

Now that we are done with the list and the pagination, we add the page into the application's navigation flow. Go to the NAVIGATION tab and select Add item. Select the page we have developed. Give it a meaningful name and assign it a suitable icon.

 

Previewing and testing the updated app

Navigate to LAUNCH and select Open preview portal. Select Open web preview and use the latest version to Open preview. If all is configured correctly, we should see a list of 10 products and are able to use the buttons to navigate the pages.

 

Adding Create, Update and Delete

In the previous section, we have covered the Read operations for this CRUD app. It is now time to add the functionality for Create, Update and Delete.

Navigate to the pages overview and select ADD NEW PAGE. Provide a name for the page. In this case, I am assigning it the name "Create new product".

Once the page is opened in UI Canvas editor, switch to the variables and create a new data variable for storing the new record. Add a new data variable based on Products. As for the data variable type, select New data record. For the name of the data variable, I am using "newProduct".

On the UI Canvas, drag and drop a Button and a few Input fields. Bind the value of the input fields, to the newProduct record items. 

For the Create button, we need to add some more logic.

On receiving the component tap event, we create a new data record in Products. Upon success, we briefly show a toast message and navigate back to the previous page. Upon failure, we will show an alert and stay on the current page.

As for the configuration of Create record, we use the following custom object:

For ID, we will generate UUID using a formula. The properties name and price are assigned the corresponding values in the newProduct record.

Adding a detail page for Read, Update and Delete

Following the master-detail UI pattern, we will now add a new page that shows the details of a selected record.

Navigate to the pages overview and select ADD NEW PAGE. Provide a name for the page. In this case, I am assigning it the name "Product Details".

Once the page is opened in UI Canvas editor, switch to the variables and create a new data variable for the storing the selected record.

We add a page parameter called productID of type UUID.

Add a new data variable based on Products. As for the data variable type, select New data record. For the name of the data variable, I am using "selectedProduct", and the ID will be the previously created productID.

Going back to the UI canvas, we drag and drop a few buttons and input fields.

For the Delete button, we add some more logic. When the Delete button is pressed, we ask for confirmation using a dialog. If confirmed, we delete the record with ID productID from Products. When successful, we navigate back to the previous page. If deletion fails, we show an alert to inform the user.

For the Update button, we add a similar logic flow. When the Update button is pressed, we update the record with ID productID of Products. When successful, we navigate back to the previous page. If the update fails, we show an alert to inform the user.

Now that we are done with the Product Details page, we will configure the page navigation for accessing the new pages we have added to the app.

Adding page navigation

We go back to the newly created Products List page, and drag and drop a button onto the page. I've also removed the Title and the Text on top of the page to make room for a button.

Upon receiving the Component tap event, we open the 'Create new product' page, and provide the selected item as parameter.

 

After testing the app in preview, and are happy with the new page, we can remove the old Products List and replace it with the new one. We also rename the new page and move it to the top of the navigation list.


Resolving an interoperability issue between SAP Build Apps and CAP version 8

At the time of writing this blog post, we have identified an issue with OData create and update operations initiated from SAP Build Apps. The data service based on CAP CDS version 8 does no longer support the property odata.type and reports error 400 if you do try to use it. We are working to resolve this issue, while making sure all other integrations keep working as expected.

If you run into this issue, you can opt for using the old protocol adapter in your data service created in SAP Build Coce by configuring the following:

 

cds.features.odata_new_adapter = false

 

As a temporary workaround, you can add the following in your project's package.json file:

Once you redeploy your data service, the create and update operations will work again.
More information on the use of protocol adaptors in CAP can be found in the CAP documentation. 

Not the most efficient implementation

Is this the most perfect and efficient configuration of a CRUD app in SAP Build Apps? Not likely. That was not the goal of this blog post. I have tried to keep things simple. There is surely room for improvement, such as:

  • Trigger less OData service requests
  • Make use of entity data already received, instead of sending another query
  • Faster trigger of queries when navigating pages

Stay tuned for improvements in this area, while we are adding more features to SAP Build Apps.

The end-result on Mobile (preview app)

Below is a screen recording of the app running on my iPhone inside the SAP Build Apps app. I am showing a list of products (Read), Adding a new product (Create), checking whether the pagination counts the correct number of pages, update to price of a selected product (Update) and finally remove a selected product (Delete).

Summary – use case G

In this use case we have replaced a simple list view page with a CRUD page. Instead of modifying the existing page, we've introduced a new one, that eventually replaced the original page.

As a first step, we've added pagination. This avoids loading lot's of data coming from an S/4HANA Cloud system. 

Next, we've added a page for adding new products and add a button to navigate to this page.

Follow the master-detail UI pattern, when a user selects a product from the list, a new page will open that shows all the details of the product. In our example we have Supplier as additional information. But there is surely room for more. The detail page allows users to update the record. We've also added a button to delete the record.

What's next

In the next blog post, we will continue on the connectivity topic and move our focus to connecting to ABAP systems.

entry.title: How Winning Product Designs Leverage PLM and ERP
entry.content:
Close collaboration between engineering teams inside and outside the enterprise requires a comprehensive approach to product development.

The ability to identify, define and specify new product ideas is paramount for maintaining competitive advantage in the dynamic world of manufacturing. Engineers play a crucial role in this process, bridging the gap between concept and reality.

However, the creation of successful new products calls for a comprehensive approach that involves multiple departments and links to a wide range of enterprise processes. Selecting and defining new products requires close collaboration between teams and participants beyond the R&D or Engineering organization and even beyond the enterprise itself.

Identifying New Product Opportunities

The first step towards creating a successful product is typically the collection of ideas from the internal community, including product experts, technicians and the marketing and sales teams. In addition, external sources such as customers, partners and technology experts need to be involved to understand the needs of the market.

This idea collection process is collaborative, facilitated through a central idea repository that is ideally available as a cloud service accessible by internal and external communities worldwide.

A central idea management platform helps companies to keep track of promising new product candidates, share and develop product ideas with the community and discover synergies between ideas from various business units within the company.

Valuable input can also be gained through a continuous review of revenues, cost and quality performance of the company’s existing product portfolio. Declining sales numbers or increasing production costs can indicate the need for a product re-design or new product generation. For this reason, product managers need to be able to retrieve a 360-degree product view that includes multiple product aspects such as product revenues, quality and compliance information as well as development and production costs.

Such a 360-degree view requires connecting a Product Lifecycle Management (PLM) system to the company’s enterprise resource planning (ERP) system and its associated accounting, production and supply processes.

Managing so many product details can quickly overwhelm innovation and product management teams, but using Artificial Intelligence (AI) can help support these teams by scanning and identifying similar ideas, connecting experts across the company and identifying synergies between development projects.

This is why idea management is not just a spreadsheet of good product ideas: it’s a managed business process that requires collaboration, integration with ERP and input from across the company and beyond, blended with a bit help from AI.

 

Screenshot of Product Innovation Campaigns with an application within the SAP PLM solutions portfolio.

 

Selecting the Best Product Ideas with Portfolio Management

Once a promising product idea has been identified, it’s time to ask for budget and resources to develop and bring the product to the market. In a highly innovative company, there will be hundreds of new product ideas all competing for the same limited resources and budgets. How do you ensure the right projects are picked to avoid burning money and resources with R&D projects that deliver low value or which do not meet the overall corporate strategy?

Companies with large product portfolios—e.g. in consumer products, life sciences or high-tech—have established a portfolio management process for this purpose. This process helps the product managers at those enterprises analyze new product investments as well as compare costs, risks and benefits for each development project. In addition, product managers can also use scoring methods to generate a ranked list of project ideas and evaluate how these investments fit to corporate strategy and goals.

In a highly dynamic environment, a structured portfolio management process is important to reduce the risk of poor investment decisions, resulting in a highly effective R&D team that can steadily increase its output of successful and sustainable product launches.

As a pre-condition for running an efficient portfolio management process, it is crucial for a company to define and communicate clear, strategic objectives and develop a prioritization framework that considers factors such as strategic alignment, potential return on investment, resource availability, market demand and risk.

The portfolio process requires product managers to evaluate the feasibility of each project proposal by assessing its technical, operational, financial and market viability. In addition, costs and benefits associated with each project need to be estimated before the investment decision is made. These estimates will serve later as the baseline for monitoring planned product development costs against actual value provided by the accounting department.

Last but not least, portfolio management aims to check the organization’s resource capacity, including personnel, budget and infrastructure. It ensures that selected projects can be adequately supported without overburdening the organization. Here, project portfolio management requires accurate insights into workforce availability, considering individual information such as sickness, leave and working hours. Ideally, a resource planning tool should be able to retrieve this information from the HR system and match availability against the project demands.

 

Capacity Planning with SAP’s Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management and SAP Analytics Cloud.

 

An efficient portfolio decision process requires multi-dimensional input from various teams and processes across the company and beyond. Portfolio management is a true ERP discipline that is fueled with project and product ideas and information from sales, finance, manufacturing and HR.

Executing and Monitoring Product Development Projects

Translating portfolio decisions into executable tactical project plans represents a critical breaking point in the innovation process. How do you ensure that agreed upon timelines, budgets and resource commitments are available to successfully initiate and execute the project on the operational level? It’s been observed that, in many organizations, portfolio management is managed through an isolated  IT solution, decoupled from the tactical project management system and ERP processes.

This increases the risk that projects will start to deviate from their initial plan and goals, failing to deliver the outcomes that have been agreed on and approved by the portfolio stakeholder. Thus, it is crucial that every investment case that is approved within a portfolio management solution is firmly linked to an operational project plan. This enables the stakeholders to track project execution and understand how changes impact the initial business case.

By establishing a systematic link between strategic planning and operational execution, the project management office can reduce the need for manual interactions and automate a large part of the reporting process. This enables the project management office to handle large project portfolios and quickly generate comprehensive and accurate project reports at any time.

A real, live example from a German automotive supplier shows that automating the synchronization between the strategic portfolio and the tactical project level shortened the time for generating stakeholder reports from several weeks to a few hours.

 

Manage projects company-wide from a centralized repository and keep the portfolio on track – from forecasting and planning to accounting and closure with SAP.

 

The growing complexity of products, driven not only by new technologies but also by an increasing amount of sustainability, product compliance and commercial requirements leads to more complex development projects.

In the past, a local team would be sufficient to develop and launch a single product. Nowadays, a full armada of experts spread across the globe must be involved. This requires additional efforts in terms of coordination and synchronization between project team members. In such virtual work environments, team members need to be proactively informed about expected deliverables, tasks and timelines. In addition, team members in remote locations need ways to easily report back progress, changes and upcoming issues in the project.

A global cloud-based project platform can help to facilitate the collaboration between project members beyond silos in engineering and stakeholders in and outside of an enterprise by providing direct access to project plans, task lists and status reports from anywhere in the world. In addition, the ability to link project tasks directly to the engineering work—such as CAD drawings, specifications, requirements lists, etc.—helps the project manager keep track of deliverables in each development stage and monitor overall project progress based on real engineering artifacts.

 

Manage and track project costs with Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management in SAP S/4HANA.

 

Design Better with PLM and ERP Together

Creating successful new products in the manufacturing industry requires a holistic approach that integrates not only research and engineering but also a wide range of ERP process, including finance/controlling, compliance, sustainability, supply chain and human resource management.

By fostering cross-departmental collaboration through a PLM solution that is deeply integrated into a company’s ERP system and using early supplier networks, enterprises are able to increase their efficiency in designing winning products for highly competitive markets.

By leveraging a natively integrated PLM+ERP environment, engineers and designers can efficiently define their portfolio to develop and deliver sustainable and profitable products that meet customer needs.

Read more in the article Striking the Perfect Balance and on the SAP website.

entry.title: MDG Cloud ready Mode: Integration of S/4HANA with SAP Build process automation & SAP Build work zone
entry.content:
Hi All,

In October 2023, SAP launched a new operational mode in MDG "CLOUD-READY MODE" for S/4 HANA 2023, following the principles of ABAP cloud and clean core methodology. Cloud-ready mode is only available for Business partners with Product domains in the MDG future roadmap. 

I briefly wrote about how this mode works in a previous blog post, hence if you have missed that you find the link below

Introducing MDG cloud ready mode for Business partners in S/4 HANA 2023

INTRODUCTION:

To setup cloud ready mode, we have following pre-requisites:

  1. An S/4 HANA 2023 private cloud edition system.
  2. A BTP tenant with at least one subaccount.
  3. Running instances of SAP build process automation, SAP Build workzone (standard Edition) and cloud connector

In this blog post, I will provide a detailed step by step approach to setup SAP build process automation and backend S/4 HANA 2023 for cloud ready mode.

MAIN CONTENT

BTP configuration:

  1.  We start by subscribing to SAP build process automation and SAP build workzone( standard edition ) in the BTP cockpit.
  2.  Assign the below role collections to your user. 

  3. On the destination tab in BTP cockpit, create the WM_CF_SPACE_PROVIDER destination. This destination will be used to import the live process packages in SAP build process automation.

    Note: The cloud foundry API end point and the URL for the destination should match as given in the SAP help portal. The user can be a generic user who doesn't have 2 factor authentication enabled. The details can be found Default Identity Provider | SAP Help Portal

     
  4. From Instances tab, launch the SAP build process automation lobby and import the Master data process approval & visualization project from the Store.

  5. Activate the 5 visibility scenarios, which we will use at a later stage.

     

  6. In the Instances tab in BTP cockpit, check sap_processautomation instance is created. Click on this and create a service key for the backend S4 HANA system.

     

  7. Assign MDG-specific BTP role collections to your user.

  8. The next step would be to setup the SAP build workzone. For the sap_processautomation instance create a service key for the workzone. 

  9. Once the key is created, create a destination for workzone. For details on how various parameters of the destination will be set, please refer to Configure SAP Build Process Automation Destinations | SAP Help Portal

     

  10. From the instances & subscription tag, launch the SAP build workzone standard edition application.
  11. In workzone, navigate to the content manager and choose the below 5 apps.
  12.  Create a local copy of the Visibility Scenario Dashboard app, change the title to Process Path Overview, and provide the navigation parameter scenarioID with the value com.sap.mdg.pv.ProcessVis.

     

  13. Create a group MDG and enable the My inbox and process path overview apps
  14. Assign the permissions for this 5 apps to everyone.
  15. Create a site with the same alias name as the group we created in step 13.

     

  16. Create the below destinations in BTP for your workflow and process path overview. Configure BTP Destinations | SAP Help Portal

     

S/4 HANA on-premise configuration:

Cloud ready mode should be activated in the S/4 HANA system, once activated classic MDG related apps for mass processing and consolidation will stop working and the new consolidation & mass processing apps for S/4 HANA 2023 needs to be used.

1.  Create an SM59 destination in S/4 HANA system and provide the host by pasting the same hostname as the MDG workzone site. The path prefix should be /site/mdg.

2. Create an entry in the SM30 view /UI2/V_ALIASMAP as below.

3. Go to MDGIMG-> cloud-ready mode and follow the path as per below screenshot to begin with integration of Build process automation with MDG. The subsequent steps will cover all the configurations in detail.

4. In Maintain the destination to an external server, create a destination MDG_BTP_SBPA_API. The host will be same as the API endpoint for the service key created in step6 of the BTP configuration section. You have to set SSL to active.

5. Now we maintain the OAuth Client 2.0 for secure communication between SBPA & S4 HANA.

Create a new profile configuration:
For the OAuth 2.0 client profile, choose SWF_CPPA_OAUTH_PROFILE. Enter a configuration name, for example, the name of the subaccount of your service.
For the client ID, enter the JSON value $.uaa.clientid from your service key card.
